title: icy venezuelan cassava pilaf
description: a refreshing twist on traditional venezuelan cassava dish, combined with yuzu-cured flavors and crunchy texture.
instructions: bring 2 cups of water to a boil in a medium saucepan over high heat.
add 1 tsp of salt and 1 tsp of dried parsley. stir to combine.
reduced heat to low and gently stir in 2 cups of cassava flour. let cook for 5 minutes or until pilaf is fluffy and water is mostly absorbed.
transfer the pilaf to a large bowl and let cool to room temperature.
in a separate pan, caramelize 1 chicken breast (see yuzu-cure recipe below).
in a separate bowl, mix together 1/4 cup of yuzu juice and chopped scallions.
in a large serving dish, arrange the cooled pilaf, chicken breast, and yuzu-scallion mixture.
garnish with dried cilantro and serve immediately.
